title: Make retryable operations idempotent
description: Any operation a caller or queue may retry must produce the same end state whether run once or many times.
body: Use when adding any write, charge, email, or external call that a client retry, queue redelivery, or timeout could repeat. Do not assume the network delivers a result exactly once. Actions: derive a stable idempotency key (client-supplied or natural business key), record it before the side effect, and short-circuit on replay returning the stored result. Validation signals include a test that replays the same key and asserts a single effect plus identical response. Risk boundary: a side effect with no idempotency boundary on a money/notification path is a blocking finding requiring a human gate.
